title: He Sought, and Was Found
subtitle: The risen Lord appears to Peter, to Joseph in his prison, and to James who would not eat until he saw Him

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the day fall away.
Divine consolation is gained first through fear,
then through tears, then through bearing tribulation,
then through humbling oneself, through fleeing earthly comfort,
and through the contemplation of things above.
gospel: and with joy they spoke together,
and here too a great Passover was made.
To James the Lord also appeared,
and breaking the bread, He gave it to him, saying:
for the Son of Man is risen from the dead.
behold: When Mary Magdalene and her companions returned and told the disciples that the Lord had risen,
Peter, grieving that he had not seen his Lord,
and unable to rest for the vehemence of the love he bore Him,
suddenly departed from the others to seek the Lord, and went alone toward the tomb.
For he did not know where else he might seek Him.
And while he hastened on, the Lord appeared to him on the way.
Then Peter, seeing the Lord, fell to the ground,
and begged pardon for having forsaken Him, and so often denied Him;
but the Lord, forgiving his sins and consoling and comforting him, bade him strengthen his brethren.
Departing from Peter, the Lord appeared to Joseph of Arimathea, who had buried Him in his own tomb.
For when the Jews heard that Joseph had begged the body of Jesus from Pilate and had honourably buried it,
they were enraged against him, seized him that same evening, and shut him in a chamber, sealed and locked,
binding him to a column in the prison, meaning to kill him after the sabbath.
And behold, on the very day of the Resurrection, the house lifted up by four angels,
the Lord came in to him, and joyfully appeared to him;
and consoling him, He wiped his face, and gave him a kiss,
and, the seals unbroken, led him out and freed him, and brought him to his own house in Arimathea.
And James, who in the supper had vowed and sworn that he would eat no bread from that hour until he saw the Lord risen,
had tasted no food until the day of the Resurrection.
The Lord appeared, set bread before him, blessed and broke it, and gave it to him.
consider: Singularly did the Lord appear to Peter, who had singularly denied Him.
He who had wavered through fear in the Passion, the Lord came to reform.
He showed him the sin of his threefold denial forgiven, consoling him that he should not despair.
See how the Lord does not forget His own, nor forsake them utterly,
but at length consoles and helps them in His own time,
for He is the helper in due season and in tribulation.
See how James, who hungered not for the bread of substance only,
but for the bread above all substance, that is, for Jesus,
deserved to be consoled and refreshed in both.
For the Lord does not suffer those who hunger for Him, or for His sake, ever to faint with want;
but gives food to them that hope in Him, in the fitting time, and fills them with His blessing.
The soul to which the Lord deigns to show Himself in any form,
He never leaves without consolation and some saving relief.
Be Peter, then, by obeying; be James, by trampling down your vices;
be Joseph, by going from virtue to virtue and persevering in it;
and the Lord will not forget to visit you by His grace, and to console you as He knows is good for you.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, grant that I may be Peter by obeying,
and James by trampling down my vices,
and Joseph, going from virtue to virtue by increase,
and in this persevering with long-suffering;
that You, who deigned to appear to them in their distress,
may deign also often to visit me, mercifully, by Your grace.
Grant me too, that as the holy Fathers had joy in Your presence
when they were counted worthy to see You glorified after Your Resurrection,
so I also may deserve to rejoice forever in Your face with them,
in that unspeakable joy with which they now rejoice with You in heaven. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Frater mi, comede panem tuum, quia Filius hominis a mortuis resurrexit.
latinJewel.english: Brother, eat your bread, for the Son of Man is risen from the dead.
